mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 3% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when FEDFUNDS is in the easing regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.98% as of 2008-02-01 indicates active easing cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 3% do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% in February 2008, near cyclical lows before deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand. When both series decline simultaneously by > 5% month-over-month, forward equity returns turn negative over 20 trading days as consensus earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1012k units in January 2008, declining from prior peaks
failure_condition: Joint decline signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward return sign.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.23 in January 2008, showing early weakness signals
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when INDPRO is below its 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration. When both conditions occur simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 20 trading days as consumer spending and corporate earnings face headwinds.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50k and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137993k in February 2008, near cyclical peak before decline
failure_condition: Joint labor signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between PAYEMS change and UNRATE change has t-stat < 1.5 in predictive regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% annualized signals late-cycle or recessionary conditions. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as market reprices terminal growth rates and equity risk premiums widen.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 2% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11677.1B in Q3 2007, with growth decelerating into 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods when GDP growth < 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals real monetary tightness. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as real borrowing costs remain elevated despite nominal easing, constraining corporate investment and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.571 in February 2008, FEDFUNDS at 2.98%
regime_hint: monetary policy lag or real tightening regime
failure_condition: Divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not significant (p > 0.15) in predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1000k units signals housing market collapse and negative wealth effects. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as construction employment falls, consumer confidence erodes, and financial sector stress emerges from mortgage-backed asset deterioration.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1012k units in January 2008, approaching critical threshold
regime_hint: housing market downturn or financial stress regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of months when HOUST < 1000k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
